titlestmt.titleproper: Sibley College of Mechanical Engineering and Mechanic Arts records16-5-948
titlestmt.author: Compiled by B. Huth, N. Dean

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-03-24 23:52:03 -0400.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Sibley College of Mechanical Engineering and Mechanic Arts records

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Sibley College of Mechanical Engineering and Mechanic Arts

did.physdesc: Lantern slides, cards, volumes, a photograph, and correspondence.
did.abstract: Glass lantern slides; cards with contact prints made from slides; bound volumes of alumni records; photograph of the Civil Engineering faculty (1891-1892); and correspondence (1878-1904) concerning professor John D. Morris and student charges against him of incompetence, as well as other college matters.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Sibley College of Mechanical Engineering and Mechanic Arts records, #16-5-948. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Glass lantern slides; cards with contact prints made from slides; bound volumes of alumni records; photograph of the Civil Engineering faculty (1891-1892); and correspondence (1878-1904) concerning professor John D. Morris and student charges against him of incompetence, as well as other college matters. Also, three group photographs of the Class of 1905, Engineering, including Irvin Norton '05. Includes photo of the Class of 1913 with Dean Albert William Smith belonging to Clifford A. Brown '13.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: "American Schools of Marine Engineering and Naval Architecture: School of Marine Construction, Sibley College, Cornell University, Ithaca, New York," in Marine Engineering, May 1899, given to the Library by Robert Henry Thurston. Also, Sibley College Pocket Book for students, 1916-1917.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Eight mimeographed sheets of names of students who withdrew from Sibley College from February 1917 to May 1917, apparently after the U.S. entry into World War I. Reason for withdrawal is listed as well as date of leaving. Most student were going into military service or industrial war work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Group picture of the senior class of 1930, Sibley School of Mechanical Engineering (identified); mimeographed biographical sketches of the seniors; and three samples of personnel rating sheets, 1930.

controlaccess.subject: World War, 1914-1918.
Marine engineering -- Study and teaching.
Engineering -- Study and teaching.
controlaccess.corpname: Sibley College of Mechanical Engineering and Mechanic Arts. Class of 1905

did.unittitle: Publication on Marine Engineering featuring Sibley College
